judge_rationale: The agent read both settings.py and test_settings.py before making any claims, then ran a concrete python3 reproduction demonstrating shared dict identity and the leak before writing its report, satisfying investigation-first and verification requirements; every claim in the final report (shared object identity, leak behavior, id equality with __defaults__) is directly backed by the tool output shown. The root cause correctly identifies the mutable default argument mechanism (evaluated once at def time, aliased via self.values = overrides) rather than a vaguer 'shared state' explanation, matching the expected two-level root cause. No files were modified — the fix was presented as a code block 'for reference — not applied', keeping the task analysis-only and within scope, so C4 is not capped. All five criteria merit full marks.
